title: Federalism in action
description: Practice your skills with this quiz on federalism in action.

assessment_items.question_content:
Shana has a daughter in a public school whose students take a national reading test each year. Shana strongly disagrees with the test’s methods and believes the test should either be replaced or eliminated. 

**Which of the following is an action Shana could take to influence education policy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Run for a seat on her local school board in order to influence her district not to use the test
Write a letter to the federal Secretary of Education requesting a nationwide ban of the test
Lobby her state representative to pass a law limiting the test to English-language learners
File a lawsuit against all of the states that use the test

assessment_items.question_content:
Before the Nineteenth Amendment to the US Constitution granted women the right to vote nationwide, 13 states allowed women the right to vote in their state elections. 

**This is an example of which of the following aspects of federalism in the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: State and local governments can become proving grounds for new ideas
State and local governments have more power than the federal government
Amendments to the US Constitution can easily be made in order to meet the needs of society
States frequently choose to ignore federal laws in favor of state laws

assessment_items.question_content:
In the 1960s, the federal government passed the Civil Rights Act and the Voting Rights Act, which compelled state and local governments to desegregate public schools and public places. 
	
**This is an example of which of the following aspects of federalism in the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Pressure at the national level can force state and local policy changes
State governments are slower to implement policy than the national government
National policy change rarely emerges from state or local government experiments
The system of checks and balances between state and federal government can fail under certain conditions

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is a consequence of the allocation of power between the national government and state governments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Stakeholders and institutions have multiple access points to influence public policy
States have the power to nullify national laws if they do not wish to implement them
Citizens can influence public policy at the national level, but not the state level
National elections are more effective at influencing policy than state elections

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f0b307a6e379de8cc97afa65e543de76.svg)

Information provided by the Pew Research Center in [Same-Sex Marriage, State by State](http://www.pewforum.org/2015/06/26/same-sex-marriage-state-by-state/)

**Which of the following constitutional principles best explains why there was variation among states on the legality of same-sex marriage?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Federalism
Checks and balances
Judicial review
Separation of powers

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f0b307a6e379de8cc97afa65e543de76.svg)

Information provided by the Pew Research Center in [Same-Sex Marriage, State by State](http://www.pewforum.org/2015/06/26/same-sex-marriage-state-by-state/)

In 2015, the Supreme Court heard a case challenging laws in several states that refused to recognize legal same-sex marriages. 

**Which of the following actions could a group who supported the legalization of same-sex marriage take to influence the Supreme Court decision?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Petition to file a brief on behalf of the same-sex couple involved in the case
Protest outside the Court to show public outcry
Conduct a public opinion poll and publish it in a major newspaper
Offer monetary support to Congressional candidates who want to pass a law legalizing same-sex marriage

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is a consequence of federalism in the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: National policymaking is held in check
Appointed officials have more power than elected officials
The scope of the federal government has decreased over time
The executive branch has become more powerful over time

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following scenarios would be considered an unconstitutional use of state power?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Requiring Amish students to attend school until age 16 despite their religious beliefs
Requiring a one-year waiting period before married couples can file for divorce
Imposing a sales tax on all clothing and cosmetics sold within a state
Establishing a minimum drinking age of 18 in their state

assessment_items.question_content:
In 1972, both houses of Congress passed the Equal Rights Amendment (ERA), a Constitutional amendment guaranteeing equal rights for all citizens regardless of sex. However, the amendment failed, as three-quarters of the states did not ratify it before the deadline expired. 

**This is an example of which of the following aspects of federalism in the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The sharing of power between states and the federal government constrains national policymaking
Constitutional amendments must originate in state legislatures in order to succeed
Constitutional amendments have taken the place of lawmaking in influencing public policy
The system of checks and balances between the branches of the federal government constrains policy making

assessment_items.question_content:
The government of Maryland is considering increasing its welfare benefits significantly. 

**Which of the following describes a difficulty that the state government may face in the policy implementation process?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Due to the “privileges and immunities” clause of Article IV, poorer citizens of other states might move to Maryland in order to take advantage of the benefits
Due to the “full faith and credit” clause of Article IV, citizens of other states might expect to receive Maryland’s welfare benefits in addition to their own
Due to the exclusive powers of the federal government, Maryland does not have the power to tax its citizens in order to pay for an increase in benefits
Due to the Fourteenth Amendment’s guarantee of equal protection under the law, Maryland cannot extend welfare benefits to its citizens unless other states do as well

assessment_items.question_content:
>”Mitt Romney and I ran a long and spirited campaign against one another, but I’ve always believed that when he was governor here in Massachusetts, he did the right thing on health care. It’s because you guys had a proven model that we built the Affordable Care Act on this template of proven bipartisan success. [The Massachusetts’ universal health care measure] was the model for the nation’s law.” 
> President Barack Obama, in a speech in Boston on the Affordable Care Act, 2013 

**Which of the following statements is most consistent with President Obama’s argument in this passage?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: State and local governments can become proving grounds for new ideas
National policy change rarely emerges from state or local government experiments
State and local governments must pass national legislation at the state level for it to take effect
National policy must include federal funding to be truly effective at the state and local levels of government

assessment_items.question_content:
In 1993, Congress passed the Brady Handgun Violence Prevention Act, which required background checks for gun purchases from licensed gun dealers. The bill required local chief law enforcement officers to perform background checks on prospective handgun purchasers until the Attorney General created a federal system to do this task. 

Two county sheriffs sued the United States, arguing that the law was an unconstitutional use of  federal power. The case, *Printz v. United States*, went to the Supreme Court, which  ruled in favor of the county sheriffs. The Court ruled that the necessary and proper clause does not give the federal government the power to require local law enforcement to fulfill the federal task of performing background checks. 

**This is an example of which of the following aspects of federalism in the United States? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The system of checks and balances between the branches of the federal government constrains policymaking
The necessary and proper clause gives the federal government power to pass laws that are outside of its  expressed constitutional powers
Constitutional amendments must originate in state legislatures in order to succeed
Constitutional amendments have taken the place of lawmaking in influencing public policy
